headlines: Iran War Threatens Global Food Supply
UK Exports to US Crash 25% Post-Tariffs
Anthropic Closing In on $900bn Valuation
Apple Smashes Earnings, Cook Eyes the Exit
Alphabet Closing In on Nvidia's Crown
Whisky Tariff Win Sparks Scottish Political Spat
**Nigel Farage** faces a potential Electoral Commission investigation over an undisclosed $5 million gift that rivals say should have been declared.
**Israel** intercepted the Global Sumud aid flotilla off Gaza and detained activists, releasing all but two; organisers called it piracy.
**Spotify** is rolling out green 'Verified' badges on artist profiles to distinguish human musicians from AI-generated content.
**Exxon and Chevron** are defying White House pressure to ramp up drilling, sticking to capital discipline despite Trump's plea to cut petrol prices.
**Tuscany** wildfires have scorched over 810 hectares of forest, with Italian firefighters mobilised across multiple fronts.
Trump Pulls 5,000 Troops From Germany
Trump Claims War Powers Act Does Not Apply
EU Car Tariffs Jump to 25% as Trump Rips Up Deal
Pentagon Goes All-In on AI With Seven Firms
Berkshire Enters the Abel Era Cautiously
Holyrood Election Enters Final Stretch
**Sabastian Sawe** returned to a hero's welcome in Eldoret, Kenya after becoming the first man to run an official marathon in under two hours at the London Marathon.
**Spirit Airlines** is preparing to shut down entirely after bailout talks with bondholders collapsed this week, ending the budget carrier's post-bankruptcy survival bid.
**Musk vs. Altman** continued in court, with damaging emails and tweets surfacing as Musk spent a third day on the witness stand in his lawsuit alleging OpenAI abandoned its nonprofit mission.
**The Oscars** ruled that AI-generated performances and writing cannot win awards, drawing a hard line as studios debate how far to lean on the technology.
**Andy Burnham** has a credible plan to re-enter Westminster 'within weeks,' with allies saying he has identified seats where sitting Labour MPs would stand aside to enable a leadership bid.
Iran War Fertiliser Warning: Billions of Meals at Risk
Trump Pirates Iranian Oil Near Hormuz
Trump Hikes EU Car Tariffs to 25%
Pentagon Goes AI-First: Seven Firms Signed
BBC News Bears Deepest Cuts in 2,000 Job Cull
Holyrood Campaign: Final Week Flashpoints
**WHO** approved Coartem Baby, the first malaria treatment for newborns, in what health officials are calling a major milestone for sub-Saharan Africa.
**Uganda** is pushing a bill that would jail dissidents for up to 20 years for promoting 'foreign interests' - critics call it a copy of Russia's foreign agent laws.
**Andy Burnham** allies say he has identified Westminster seats where Labour MPs will stand aside, putting a leadership challenge on a concrete footing within weeks.
**Ultra Electronics**, a UK defence firm, agreed to pay **15 million pounds** after an SFO bribery investigation linked to contracts in Algeria and Oman.
**Meta** acquired humanoid robotics startup Assured Robot Intelligence, signalling a serious push into physical AI beyond chatbots and data centres.
